seo.title: Louisiana Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Louisiana — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license louisiana, dental assistant certification louisiana, RDA louisiana, DANB CDA louisiana, dental assistant radiography louisiana

license_types.title: Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Louisiana.

steps.title: Meet Basic Requirements
steps.description: Applicants must have a high school diploma or equivalent, and current CPR certification.

steps.title: Find a Sponsoring Dentist
steps.description: You must be currently employed by a dentist licensed in Louisiana.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete the Dental Assistant Registration application and submit it with the $100 fee to the Louisiana State Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) through the Louisiana State Board of Dentistry by certification.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Louisiana requires Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Louisiana.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Louisiana?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $100. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Louisiana does not regulate general dental assistants. EDDA certification required for expanded duties.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Louisiana
hero.dek: Louisiana Dental Assistant registration requires graduation from high school (or equivalent), CPR certification, and employment by a Louisiana licensed dentist.
